name: Breast and axillary anatomy
review_note: Nodal levels and nerve courses vary in depiction; confirm level references and nerve identities against your atlas.

figure_scopes.overview: Breast and axillary orientation with pectoralis major/minor context
figure_scopes.storyboard: Orientation -> nodal levels/sentinel pathway -> axillary vein & nerve-sparing -> preserved relationships
figure_scopes.closeup: Axillary vein with long thoracic and thoracodorsal nerves preserved

laterality: Axilla; axillary vein is the superior landmark, long thoracic nerve runs on the serratus/chest wall, thoracodorsal bundle courses posterolaterally
must_have: Breast tissue, pectoralis major/minor context, axillary vein, long thoracic nerve, thoracodorsal bundle, intercostobrachial nerve region, and level I/II/III nodal anatomy when requested.
Sentinel node path or axillary dissection levels only if requested.
spatial_rules: Axillary vein forms the superior landmark for axillary dissection anatomy.
Long thoracic nerve runs on the chest wall / serratus anterior region.
Thoracodorsal bundle courses posterior/lateral toward latissimus dorsi.

forbidden_errors.error: Long thoracic nerve confused with the thoracodorsal nerve.
Nodal levels placed without a pectoralis minor reference when level anatomy is requested.
Nerves severed or crossed through lymph node labels.

safety_concept: Preserve the long thoracic and thoracodorsal nerves; reference nodal levels to pectoralis minor.
phases: Nodal levels or sentinel pathway, if requested.
Final preserved nerve/vessel relationship, if useful.
verify: Axillary vein, long thoracic nerve, and thoracodorsal bundle are distinct.
Nodal levels are referenced to pectoralis minor when shown.
Nerve preservation relationships are visually clear.
